one_line_ask: Take our approved event wordmark, delegate roster CSV (~1,000 rows) and authored InDesign badge template, prep and vectorize the wordmark plus the four role QR marks, then run an InDesign data-merge to bind every delegate into a personalised name badge and export a print-ready imposed PDF plus a high-res proof.
brief: Northgate Dental Students' Congress 2027 is a two-day event bringing roughly 1,000 delegates from dental schools across the country together for speakers, workshops, a gala dinner and socials. Our brand identity is already signed off, and we now need the physical delegate name badges produced from our attendee list — one personalised badge per delegate. We have handed off everything the job needs: our approved event wordmark as a flat high-contrast PNG (northgate_wordmark.png); an authored InDesign badge template (badge_template.indd) laid out at 90x130mm portrait with 3mm bleed, containing real InDesign Data Merge fields (<<FullName>>, <<School>>, <<Role>>, <<QRPath>>), a placeholder frame for the vectorized wordmark, a full-width role colour-band frame, and a QR image frame; our delegate roster export (delegate_roster.csv) with one row per delegate holding full name, school, role (Delegate / Speaker / Committee / Press) and the matching role-QR filename; and four flat role-band QR marks as PNGs (one per role, each a solid-colour block with the check-in QR). The wordmark PNG is slightly oversized and off-centre for the badge frame and needs cleaning and vectorizing so it stays raz
